news.summary: The latest data reveals that the unemployment rate in the country has dropped to 4.6%, down from 5.2% the previous month. This decrease in unemployment represents around 500,000 people who have found employment. Additionally, the economy saw a growth of 2.3% in the last quarter, exceeding expectations. The stock market also experienced a significant increase, with the main index rising by 800 points. These positive economic indicators are attributing to a more optimistic outlook for the future.
news.headline: Codexis announces Q1 2025 financial results with significant numbers, without mentioning the source.

news.summary: The enrollment for Phase 3 trials of TNX-103 in patients with PH-HFpEF is set to be completed by the end of 2025. Topline data from the study is anticipated to be released in the middle of 2026.
news.headline: Tenax Therapeutics released Q1 2025 financial results. Phase 3 enrollment for TNX-103 in PH-HFpEF to end by 2025, data expected mid-2026.

news.summary: The number of confirmed cases of COVID-19 in the country has reached a record high of 15,000 in the past week. Authorities have also reported a significant increase in hospitalizations, with over 2,000 patients currently being treated for the virus. In response to the surge in cases, the government has announced new restrictions, including the closure of schools and non-essential businesses. Additionally, health officials are urging the public to follow safety guidelines and get vaccinated to help curb the spread of the virus.
news.headline: Zentalis Pharmaceuticals reveals Q1 2025 financials and operational advancements.

news.summary: A new report indicates that the global economy is projected to grow by 5.6% this year, a significant increase from the 3.9% projected earlier. However, there are concerns about rising inflation rates, with consumer prices expected to increase by 3.8%. The report also highlights that the unemployment rate is predicted to decrease to 6.2% worldwide. Despite these positive projections, there are still challenges facing the economy, including supply chain disruptions and the ongoing COVID-19 pandemic. Experts advise policymakers to remain vigilant and implement measures to tackle these challenges.
news.headline: Jade Biosciences announces Q1 2025 financial results and corporate update, highlighting significant progress.

ImmuCell focuses on developing and distributing effective products to enhance the well-being and efficiency of cattle.
news.headline: ImmuCell reports unaudited financial results for Q1 2025, affirmed growth as animal health company.

news.summary: An ongoing effort is being made to carry out analytical research in anticipation of a potential meeting with the FDA regarding Biosimilar Biological Product Development (BPD) Type 2a in the latter half of 2025.
news.headline: AEON Biopharma's Q1 2025 financial results released, preparing for FDA meeting in 2nd half 2025 for Biosimilar BPD.

news.summary: The company achieved remarkable milestones in various aspects. Total Payment Volume (TPV) reached a significant milestone of US$8 billion, marking a 53% increase Year-over-Year (YoY) and a 5% increase Quarter-over-Quarter (QoQ). In constant currency, TPV soared by 72% YoY. Revenue and gross profit also saw record highs, hitting US$217 million and US$85 million, respectively. The company's geographic reach continued to expand. Adjusted Earnings Before Interest, Taxes, Depreciation, and Amortization (EBITDA) stood at US$58 million, with the EBITDA/Gross Profit ratio at a strong 68%, reflecting efficient scaling. The company demonstrated robust cash flow, with free cash flow conversion to net income at an impressive 85%, showcasing its capacity to generate cash effectively.
news.headline: dLocal's 2025 first quarter financial results show record highs, with TPV reaching $8 billion (+53% YoY and +5% QoQ), revenue of $217 million, gross profit of $85 million, and Adjusted EBITDA of $58 million.

news.summary: Progressing IKT-001 towards a late-stage clinical trial program for the treatment of Pulmonary Arterial Hypertension. This milestone marks a significant step forward in the development of IKT-001 as a potential therapy for PAH. With promising results from earlier trials, the decision was made to move into late-stage clinical trials to further evaluate the drug's efficacy and safety. The trial program is set to involve approximately [insert number] patients across multiple sites, with a focus on assessing the drug's impact on PAH symptoms and overall quality of life for patients. This phase of clinical trials aims to provide more conclusive data on the potential benefits of IKT-001 in treating PAH and could bring the drug closer to potential approval and commercialization.
news.headline: In Q1 2025, Inhibikase Therapeutics progresses IKT-001 into late-stage clinical trial for pulmonary arterial hypertension.

The initiative, led by Forbes Global Properties, is set to create a new avenue for high-performing agents and clients across the globe. The announcement was made on May 14, 2025, in New York, ushering in a new era for luxury property transactions.
news.headline: Forbes Global Properties partners with RBN, offering luxury rewards for buyers and sellers. Aims to elevate global real estate service.

news.summary: Excelerate Energy, Inc. (NYSE: EE) based in The Woodlands, Texas, has successfully completed the acquisition of New Fortress Energy Inc.'s (Nasdaq: NFE) business in Jamaica. This recent acquisition includes the assets and operations of key facilities such as the Montego Bay liquefied natural gas (LNG) Terminal, the Old Harbour LNG Terminal, and the Clarendon combined heat and power plant. The finalized purchase agreement marks a significant milestone for Excelerate, solidifying its presence in the Jamaican energy market.
news.headline: Excelerate Energy acquires New Fortress Energy Inc.'s business in Jamaica, including LNG terminals and power plant.

news.summary: In Charlton, Massachusetts on May 14, 2025, Tree House Distillery, a branch of Tree House Brewing Company, is thrilled to announce its success at the 2025 San Francisco World Spirits Competition. The distillery secured an impressive four double gold medals at the event.
news.headline: Tree House Distillery wins 4 double gold medals at 2025 San Francisco World Spirits Competition.

news.summary: Pharmaceutical company Sanofi SA recently publicized plans to invest at least $20 billion in the U.S. by 2030, with a focus on research and development as well as domestic manufacturing. This significant investment is expected to boost job opportunities in the communities where Sanofi and its partners operate, further strengthening the U.S. supply chain. With around 13,000 employees based in the U.S., Sanofi's CEO Paul Hudson emphasized the importance of this investment in ensuring the production of essential medicines within the country. Furthermore, Sanofi aims to collaborate with other local manufacturers to support domestic medicine production. This announcement comes in the wake of U.S. President Donald Trump's executive order aimed at enhancing prescription drug manufacturing in the U.S. For more details, visit Benzinga.com.
news.headline: Sanofi plans $20 billion investment in US by 2030, boosting job creation and enhancing supply chain.CEO Paul Hudson emphasizes substantial investments for production of key medicines in US.

news.summary: Monogram Technologies Inc. has received FDA clearance for the mBôs™ TKA System, a saw-based robotic surgical system for knee surgeries. The company aims to conduct a groundbreaking trial with 102 patients in India, showcasing fully autonomous robotic surgeries. Ben Sexson, CEO, highlighted the significance of these milestones and the potential of autonomous robotics in surgical procedures. Financially, the company reported $2.3 million in R&D expenses, $44,000 in marketing expenses, and $1.0 million in general & administrative expenses for Q1 2025. Net loss was $3.2 million. Monogram Technologies plans to focus on commercializing the mBôs™ TKA System efficiently and has $13.3 million in cash reserves. Furthermore, upcoming milestones include initiating patient enrollment for robotic surgeries, starting live patient surgeries in India, launching in the US, and refining the system. The company aims to create personalized knee implants through AI-driven robotics technology. Monogram Technologies Inc. is an AI-driven robotics company with an initial focus on orthopedic surgery and aims to improve human health through innovative robotic surgical solutions.
news.headline: Monogram Technologies received FDA clearance for mBôs TKA System, set to conduct 102-patient trial in India for autonomous surgeries. Financially, the company reported a net loss of $3.2 million for Q1 2025, with cash and cash equivalents at $13.3 million. CEO Ben Sexson aims to commercialize the platform efficiently.

news.summary: CytoSorbents Corporation reported financial results for the first quarter of 2025, indicating product revenue of $8.7 million, a 3% decrease from the previous year. Gross margin was at 71%, down from 77% in Q1 2024. Operating loss improved by 17% to $3.9 million, with a net loss of $1.5 million. Adjusted net loss was $3.7 million. The company raised total proceeds of $6.8 million through a Shareholder Rights Offering and saw increased liquidity to $11.8 million. CytoSorbents also expanded globally with a new sales subsidiary in Dubai. They appointed new executives and remain committed to introducing DrugSorb-ATR to the North American market. The FDA issued a denial letter regarding DrugSorb-ATR's De Novo Request, prompting the company to engage in the formal appeal process. Health Canada's review for the same product remains ongoing. The company's First Quarter 2025 Earnings Conference Call details further financial information and future outlook. Through their blood purification technologies, CytoSorbents addresses critical conditions in the ICU and cardiac surgery, with a focus on reducing medical complications. For further information on CytoSorbents Corporation, visit their website.
news.headline: CytoSorbents reported Q1 2025 revenue of $8.7M, operating loss improved by 17% ($3.9M), and net loss was $1.5M. Total cash at $13.1M. Expanding business globally with 70 countries distribution.FDA issued denial letter for DrugSorb-ATR, exploring appeal for approval.

news.summary: The Metals Company Inc. provided their first quarter results, including total liquidity of $43.8 million as of March 31, 2025, with $2.3 million in cash. They announced a registered direct offering of $37 million, issuing 12.3 million common shares at $3.00 each. The company reported a net loss of $20.6 million for Q1 2025. TMC USA submitted the first-ever application for a commercial recovery permit for deep-sea minerals in the U.S. They also welcomed a new executive order signed by President Trump supporting domestic supply for critical minerals. Rutger Bosland joined as Chief Innovation and Offshore Technology Officer. Additionally, TMC and PAMCO achieved a milestone in nodule processing, smelting 450 tonnes into NiCuCo alloy and Mn silicate products. The company has total assets of $64.5 million and liabilities of $81.2 million as of March 31, 2025. TMC is exploring lower-impact critical metals from seafloor polymetallic nodules and has conducted extensive research on environmental and social impacts. They will hold a conference call on May 14, 2025, to discuss these results and updates. Gerry Barron, CEO of TMC, emphasized the importance of deep-sea mineral development for U.S. industrial future and highlighted the company's strategic investments and operational milestones.
news.headline: TMC reported Q1 2025 financial highlights including $43.8m liquidity and $20.6m net loss, announces $37m strategic investment for deep-sea mineral development and submits applications for recovery permits with resources estimated at 15.5m tonnes nickel, 12.8m tonnes copper, 2m tonnes cobalt, and 345m tonnes manganese. CEO testifies to House Committee on Natural Resources. Total assets at $64.48m and total liabilities at $81.25m.

news.summary: scPharmaceuticals Inc. reported net FUROSCIX revenue of $11.8 million in the first quarter of 2025, a 93% increase from the first quarter of 2024. The company launched FUROSCIX for Chronic Kidney Disease in April 2025 and is on track for an Autoinjector sNDA submission in Q3 2025 to reduce treatment time to less than ten seconds. As of March 31, 2025, the company had cash and cash equivalents of $57.5 million. In the first quarter of 2025, approximately 13,900 doses of FUROSCIX were filled, representing a 73% increase from the previous year. The company's total shares outstanding were 50,283,925 as of March 31, 2025. Research and development expenses were $4.6 million, and selling, general and administrative expenses were $21.4 million for the first quarter of 2025. scPharmaceuticals reported a net loss of $19.7 million in the first quarter of 2025. Investor conference call and webcast were scheduled for May 14th, 2025. FUROSCIX is indicated for edema in adult patients with chronic heart failure or CKD. Excessive use can lead to fluid, electrolyte, and metabolic abnormalities. The most common adverse reactions include site and skin reactions. scPharmaceuticals aims to revolutionize cardiorenal healthcare through patient-centric innovations and improving global health.
news.headline: scPharmaceuticals Inc. reported $11.8M FUROSCIX revenue in Q1 2025, 93% annual growth with $57.5M cash, aiming for sNDA submission in Q3 for the new autoinjector.

news.summary: Journey Medical Corporation reported $13.1 million in revenue for the first quarter of 2025. The commercial launch of Emrosi (40 mg Minocycline Hydrochloride Modified-Release Capsules) started strong, with initial prescriptions filled in late March 2025. Phase 3 clinical trial results for Emrosi were published in JAMA Dermatology. The company announced a net loss of $4.1 million for the quarter. Gross margin increased to 64% in Q1 2025. At the end of March 2025, the company had $21.1 million in cash. Recent highlights include the appointment of a new COO, distribution to pharmacies, and Emrosi's inclusion in the National Rosacea Society's Treatment Algorithms. Journey Medical hosted a conference call on May 14, 2025, to discuss financial results and recent developments. Financially, the company remains in a strong position with improvements in gross margin and operating costs. Visit www.journeymedicalcorp.com for more information.
news.headline: Journey Medical reports $13.1M revenue in Q1 2025 with $21.1M cash on hand; Emrosi launch for rosacea treatment successful.

news.summary: Basel, 14 May 2025 - In a recent update, Roche revealed that the FDA has granted approval for the VENTANA® MET (SP44) RxDx Assay. This marks a groundbreaking advancement as it becomes the initial companion diagnostic tool sanctioned to assist in identifying MET (c-Met) protein levels in patients with Non-Squamous Non-Small Cell Lung Cancer (NSQ-NSCLC). Patients with this condition who exhibit MET protein expression may now qualify for treatment utilizing AbbVie’s c-Met-focused medication Emrelis™ (telisotuzumab vedotin-tllv). This development highlights a significant milestone in the field, offering a targeted therapeutic option to a specific group of patients.
news.headline: FDA approves Roche's VENTANA MET (SP44) RxDx Assay as 1st companion diagnostic for non-squamous NSCLC patients, enabling treatment with Emrelis.

news.summary: On May 14, 2025, in Mechelen, Belgium, Galapagos NV (GLPG), a global biotechnology company listed on Euronext & NASDAQ, revealed that fresh findings from the ongoing ATALANTA-1 Phase 1/2 study of GLPG5101 in relapsed/refractory non-Hodgkin lymphoma (R/R NHL) have been approved for oral presentations at the 2025 European Hematology Association (EHA) Congress happening on June 12-15, 2025, in Milan, Italy, and the subsequent International Conference on Malignant Lymphoma (ICML) in Lugano, Switzerland. This news signifies a significant leap in the company's dedication to advancing patient outcomes through groundbreaking science and innovation.
news.headline: Galapagos to present promising new data from ATALANTA-1 study of CAR-T Candidate GLPG5101 at EHA and ICML 2025. #medicine #research

news.summary: Louisville, Colorado - May 14, 2025 - Biodesix, Inc., a prominent diagnostic solutions company listed on Nasdaq as BDSX, has proudly revealed its victory as a recipient of the Colorado Top Workplaces 2025 award. The prestigious Top Workplaces initiative, known for honoring organizations that prioritize their workforce, has been conducting surveys for 18 years, covering both national and 60 regional markets. The acknowledgment is a result of feedback directly from employees obtained through a survey led by the technology partner specializing in employee engagement, Energage.
news.headline: Biodesix wins Colorado Top Workplaces 2025 award by Denver Post, based on employee survey feedback through Energage, a third-party; recognizes people-first organizations.

news.summary: On May 9, 2025, BridgeBio Pharma, Inc. approved equity grants to 36 new employees totaling 101,974 shares of the company's common stock. The shares were granted in restricted stock units, with one-fourth of each employee's shares vesting on May 16, 2026, and the rest vesting quarterly thereafter. The grants were approved by the compensation committee of BridgeBio's board of directors and are part of the Amended and Restated 2019 Inducement Equity Plan. BridgeBio is a biopharmaceutical company specializing in genetic diseases and is listed on Nasdaq under the symbol BBIO.
news.headline: BridgeBio Pharma approved equity grants for 36 employees on May 9, 2025, totaling 101,974 shares with vesting details.
